questionText: The daily numbers of items, $X$, sold by an online store are modelled by $X \sim N(\mu, \sigma^2)$. On $30\%$ of days more than $500$ items are sold, and on $5\%$ of days more than $650$ items are sold. Find $\mu$ and $\sigma$, giving your answers to $3$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the model and the two given facts
steps.explanation: Daily sales are normal with unknown $\mu$ and $\sigma$. Both facts are upper-tail: sales exceed $500$ on $30\%$ of days and exceed $650$ on $5\%$ of days. We will convert both to lower-tail form.

steps.description: Convert the first condition to a lower tail
steps.explanation: The inverse normal reads from the lower tail, so ‘$30\%$ above $500$’ becomes ‘$70\%$ below $500$’.

steps.description: Standardise and find the first z-value
steps.explanation: Standardising $500$ and applying the inverse normal to $0.70$ gives a positive $z$, since $70\%>50\%$ places the point to the right of the mean.
Equating the standardised value of $500$ with $z_1$ gives the first equation.

steps.description: Convert the second condition to a lower tail
steps.explanation: Similarly ‘$5\%$ above $650$’ means ‘$95\%$ below $650$’, ready for the inverse normal.

steps.description: Standardise and find the second z-value
steps.explanation: Standardising $650$ and taking $\Phi^{-1}(0.95)$ gives $z_2 = 1.6449$, well to the right of the mean.
Equating the standardised value of $650$ with $z_2$ gives the second simultaneous equation.
Multiplying each equation by $\sigma$ puts them into ‘value minus mean equals $z$ times standard deviation’ form for elimination.
Subtracting cancels $-\mu$; the $150$-item gap corresponds to about $1.12$ standard deviations.
Dividing the sales gap by the standardised gap gives a standard deviation of about $134$ items.
From the first equation $\mu = 500 - 0.5244\,\sigma$. The mean of about $430$ items lies below $500$, consistent with sales exceeding $500$ on only $30\%$ of days.
To three significant figures the model is $X \sim N(430,\ 134^2)$ items per day.
With $\mu = 430$ and $\sigma = 134$, about $30\%$ of days exceed $500$ items and about $5\%$ exceed $650$, confirming the fit.
